sourceSummary.oneLine: Design information for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by describing device information, intended purpose, principle of operation, design stages, design results, and design site.
keyPoints: The devices are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
The models are indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
The detector uses an indirect type design with CsI scintillator and a-Si TFT panel.
The listed risk class is Class IIa.
The identified design site is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.

concepts.name: Risk Class IIa
concepts.evidence:
Risk Class of the device

Class IIa

concepts.name: Digital radiography
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

concepts.name: Auto Exposure Detection
concepts.evidence: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

concepts.name: Indirect type detector
concepts.evidence: The indirect type detector consists of a Scintillator for converting X-rays into visible light and a TFT panel for converting them into data.

concepts.name: Design validation
concepts.evidence: Validation: Validate in accordance with the design and development validation report (A-TCF-5-099) form and review whether the validation also meets the requirements considered in accordance with the above review.

concepts.name: Design verification
concepts.evidence: Verification: Based on the attached materials, confirm the final effectiveness of product requirements such as product performance and safety in the design and development effectiveness check report (A-TCF-5-100).

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText:
Product name

Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The model names are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
The device risk class is Class IIa.
evidence.evidenceText:
Risk Class of the device

Class IIa

evidence.claim: The devices are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The detector consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics that convert read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The detectors support auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The devices are intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and support real-time diagnosis and treatment planning.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The indirect type detector converts X-rays to visible light with a scintillator and converts visible light to data with a TFT panel.
evidence.evidenceText: The indirect type detector consists of a Scintillator for converting X-rays into visible light and a TFT panel for converting them into data.

evidence.claim: The sensor type is a-Si TFT and the scintillator is CsI.
evidence.evidenceText:
Sensor Type

a-Si TFT

Scintillator

CsI

evidence.claim: The detector energy range is 40 to 150 kV.
The Wi-Fi specification listed is IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac at 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz.
The IP range listed is IP56.
The communication interface listed is 1Giga-bit Ethernet.
The trigger modes are Normal Mode and AED Mode.
evidence.evidenceText:
Trigger Mode

Normal Mode

AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode)

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is identified as a design site for design, manufacture, service, and sales.
